description_long: This is a partially destroyed, pink and white airplane fuselage with significant structural damage, including a missing nose cone, exposed interior, and broken-off sections of the wings and tail. The fuselage is cylindrical with a row of rectangular windows along each side, and the cockpit area is visible at the front. The wings are low-mounted and straight, with two propeller engines attached, both of which show bent or broken propeller blades. The tail section is truncated, exposing internal framework and wiring, and the landing gear is not visible, suggesting it may be missing or retracted. The paint is predominantly pink on the lower fuselage and wings, with white on the upper fuselage and tail, and some gray and metallic details on the engines and exposed interior. The overall appearance suggests this airplane is a derelict or crashed vehicle, likely serving as a dramatic urban obstacle or set piece.
description: A damaged pink and white airplane fuselage with broken wings, exposed interior, and bent propeller engines, missing its nose and tail sections.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the broken nose, cockpit windows, and bent propellers on the left wing.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the row of windows, missing nose, and exposed interior at the rear.
description_view_2: Rear-left view displaying the open, truncated tail section and the underside of the right wing.
description_view_3: Front-right view focusing on the cockpit, right wing with damaged propellers, and the overall fuselage shape.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles; wings are partially broken.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted on the wings, each with a visible propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer, though the tail section is truncated and partially missing.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely missing or retracted due to damage.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows at the front of the fuselage.
